name: Yacimiento de Atapuerca — circular through the Montes de Oca
name_original: Yacimiento de Atapuerca — circular por los Montes de Oca
region_intro: Hiking around Atapuerca offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, significant wetlands, and ancient forests. The region is home to the Sierra de Atapuerca, providing scenic backdrops for trails, and features unique geological formations. Hikers can explore areas rich in prehistoric human history, with routes often passing through or near the UNESCO World Heritage archaeological sites. This blend of natural beauty and historical significance creates a varied terrain for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Atapuerca The most popular hiking route is Camino de Santiago — circular by San Juan de Ortega, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic Camino de Santiago, passing by the Monastery and Church of Saint John of Ortega. Another top favourite among local hikers is Yacimiento de Atapuerca — circular through the Montes de Oca, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This trail offers expansive views through the Montes de Oca and explores the area around the Atapuerca Archaeological Site. Local hikers also love the Monte de la Pedraja Memorial – San Juan de Ortega loop from Valdefuentes (Fuente del Carnero), a 15.5 miles (24.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Atapuerca is defined by rolling hills, ancient archaeological sites, and sections of the Camino de Santiago. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Atapuerca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Atapuerca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Atapuerca?
A: Atapuerca offers a range of hiking difficulties. You'll find everything from moderate walks, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042287038/camino-de-santiago-circular-by-san-juan-de-ortega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago — circular by San Juan de Ortega&lt;/a&gt;, to more challenging routes. Overall, there are 13 easy, 34 moderate, and 9 difficult trails available, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Atapuerca?
A: Yes, Atapuerca has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails typically have minimal elevation gain and cover shorter distances, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without strenuous effort.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Atapuerca?
A: Hiking in Atapuerca allows you to experience diverse natural features, including the rolling hills of the Sierra de Atapuerca, significant wetlands like the Lagunas of Atapuerca (a haven for migratory birds), and beautiful forests. The region is also known for its ancient caves and geological formations.

Q: Are there any significant historical or archaeological landmarks along the trails?
A: Absolutely. Atapuerca is a UNESCO World Heritage site, and many trails pass through or near the famous archaeological sites, offering a glimpse into human evolution. You might also encounter sections of the historic Camino de Santiago, such as the path leading to the Cruz de Matagrande, or historical stops like the Monastery and Church of Saint John of Ortega. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major highlight.

Q: Where can I find the best viewpoints in Atapuerca?
A: Many trails in Atapuerca offer excellent viewpoints, particularly those that ascend the rolling hills. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042259048/yacimiento-de-atapuerca-circular-through-the-montes-de-oca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de Atapuerca — circular through the Montes de Oca&lt;/a&gt; route provides expansive views across the Montes de Oca. You can also enjoy stunning vistas of the lagoons and mountains from the 16th-century church in Atapuerca village.

Q: Is parking available near the hiking areas or archaeological sites?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, the Atapuerca Archaeological Site and surrounding villages typically offer designated parking areas for visitors. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Atapuerca?
A: Atapuerca is suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring. Summers can be warm, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for hiking, especially on less exposed trails.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails suitable for children in Atapuerca?
A: Yes, Atapuerca has trails that are suitable for families with children. These are typically shorter, less strenuous routes with interesting features like the botanical trail around the archaeological sites, which can engage younger hikers with local flora and fauna.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Atapuerca?
A: Many outdoor areas in Spain, including hiking trails, are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, specific rules may apply to archaeological sites or protected natural areas. It's always best to check local regulations for particular trails or sites you plan to visit.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Atapuerca?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Atapuerca. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042287038/camino-de-santiago-circular-by-san-juan-de-ortega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago — circular by San Juan de Ortega&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.1-mile loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042259048/yacimiento-de-atapuerca-circular-through-the-montes-de-oca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de Atapuerca — circular through the Montes de Oca&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular option.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Atapuerca?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique blend of natural beauty and profound historical significance, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to walk sections of the Camino de Santiago.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Atapuerca offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de la Pedraja Memorial – San Juan de Ortega loop from Valdefuentes (Fuente del Carnero)&lt;/a&gt; is a 15.5-mile trail with significant elevation gain, often taking nearly 7 hours to complete. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Agés – Matagrande Cross loop from Atapuerca&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
In the municipality of Atapuercas there is an archaeological site of primary importance, containing a large quantity of hominid bones dating back to over 400,000 years ago. For this reason the site has been included among the world heritage sites.
 The archaeological park can be visited from Monday to Friday (from 9 to 17), Saturdays and public holidays (from 9 to 14)
Atapuerca is a town and a municipality located in the province of Burgos and is known worldwide for hosting the Atapuerca sites, the cradle of the first European.
